Comment:
"""I love this couple. I pray the Lord continues I did IVF 10 times (10th time was the charm, praise God!) so I know and understand their story. Everyone's journey is different. We adopted a baby who I can only say was sent to us by God and it was after this our 10th cycle was successful - in fact, those tests that usually take 3 minutes to show a result took less than 1 minute to show a positive result! Both my children are testimonies and constant and daily reminders that God is faithful and He knows the plans He has for you, plans for an expected end. Thankfully, I am someone who doesn't really care what people say and quite quickly shut people up whenever they talked out of turn. A lot of the pressure was from myself, though. My family was supportive and my husband's family were the best. Like Adesua, I do not understand why infertility is such a hidden thing, or shameful. I share my story as the Spirit moves me, with strangers sometimes, in varying ways. I did make a promise to God that I would obey His voice and be an encouragement to whomever he sent my way. For anyone waiting on the Lord, have faith and be strong. As He raised Lazarus from the dead, so shall He breathe life into every "dead" situation in your life, be you Christian, Muslim, Jew, whatever religion. Just have faith in the One who created you.!"""

Comment:
"""The devil said I should sleep is late but I told him no that I'm gonna give my testimony.... The time in Nigeria right now is 1:15am April 8 2021 and that's when the miracle I'm about to share took place.. I started watching this video around 11pm 7th April and finished around 12 or thereabout cos I didn't look at the Time when I finished.... So I decided to go and pee and then sleep but before I was about living the room to pee, my sister came in and I immediately start telling her about this video, how #banky and wife got their miracle.... And I told her the words that #banky said in this video "Many people only see God as 911, only when you are in need or trouble that's when you remember God... that's not right.. You have to give God praise anytime of your life, whether in the good or bad times you have to give him the sacrifice of praise" and I also told my sister that #banky also said that God's love language to us is FAITH... #Note: not that I didn't know this words #banky said but for the past weeks I and my mum are really sick and i totally forgot to thank God for life instead I was only focused on asking My creator God why is he letting this happen to me and my mum (Choi my God forgive me o) not knowing that my God simply wants me to praise him in my present condition .... So hearing #banky and his wife made it more like a deep meaning and an eye opener to me... After talking to my sister, I proceeded to go and pee.. Now here's where the devil came to shame himself... But I got the victory✌️ As I was going to pee, my mum came out of her room in serious pain holding her eyes and really wailing in pain cos she have been having this eye issues for over a month now.... Then immediately devil said *oya na lemme see whether thanking God will stop your mum's eye pain*... As I was peeing (cos my mum have already pour water on her head sitting on the dinning chair with the ceiling fan over head so the pain can atleast stop) I started singing *Chineke na eme mma imela ekele diri gi imela Chineke na agwo oria imela ekele diri gi imela* in igbo tongue meaning.. Jesus the doer of Good I thank you, all praises belong to you i thank you Jesus the healer of sick I thank you, all praises belong to you I thank you* So I continued signing this song with strong faith in my heart, came out of the rest room wash my hands and straight to the dinning where my mum was.... I was singing this song in repetition telling my mum to have faith and always profess she's healed that God want us to thank him in this situation right now.... I continued singing and thanking God I never said lord heal my mum or please lord stop the pain.... Instead I said lord I thank you for this pain right now cos I know you're about to prove to the devil that you are the only almighty God who heals... Was just repeating, admonishing my mum and just telling her the words of faith and encouraging her lo and the people.... THE PAIN STOPPED! YES! YOU HEARD RIGHT, IT STOPPED OH! THANK YOU Jesus.... Infact I will stop here cos this post is already longer than expected... You know what I will start my channel with the testimony cos I wanna tell the word there's power, healing, breakthrough and more in worshipping and praising God. Oh yes I'm a living testimony together with #banky and wife... Thank you Jesus"""

Dataset context:
- Creator: Banky Wellington
- Video: Final Say Faith
- Matched keywords: faith

Comment:
"""I want to add something to this story. In Jewish customs, a person is declared dead after three days(there is also text that says 7 days, but I believe 3 days is the most observed). It is beautiful all that you said about them (Lazarus/Jesus). But, Jesus waited 4 days. Essentially, if Jesus had come and saved Lazarus earlier, the people would have said it was luck. They would've accredited the victory to chance and not the Savior. At four days, Jesus was in time to collect all HIS glory. I say "all" because the women of great faith (lazarus' family members) would have still acknowledged Jesus. Jesus healed Lazarus exactly on time. On time meant it could only be said that "God had done it" by everyone in every time. I hope that helps whoever. Again, this was just made to add to the story of Lazarus. We can also see another example of this with Hannah, the barren righteous woman. It was not until she had exhausted all human options (ex: modern IVF, etc) that she was blessed. God does not share HIS glory. Also, I do not want anyone to think I opposed IVF or other forms of looking for children. I support- in fact, it could be your "journey", as the Wellingtons remarked! This was made to inform you on something God has taught me before. Maybe someone will benefit from this....."""

- Matched keywords: boys; cheat; Friendzone; guy; guys; male; man; provide; relationship; scarcity; Don

Comment:
"""Women will tell you I like a man who's always available, I like when my man gives me too much attention, but it's actually a trap. It might be fun at the beginning, she will only tolerate it until she gets forced to ask you "Are you jobless? Don't you have anything to do with your life?" Too much availability yields disrespect, don't play yourself. Scarcity makes you more valuable, but being overly available decreases your value. You become more attractive to her when she knows you're taking your life seriously, you have goals you're trying to reach, you'd become a man who can easily provide for his kids and teach them how to get a life since you're goal oriented, and you're focused on chasing success. That's the type of man she wants to end up with. Some of you are always texting and calling your woman 24/7, mostly out of insecurity, hoping you take all her time so she doesn't have time for anyone else, or you seeing her everyday, hoping she doesn't have time to cheat on you. She doesn't miss you at all because you're always in her face. In a relationship, as much as it's important to be available, being too available will ruin it quickly. She'd lose attraction for you easily. Relationship aside, Most men who are in the friend zone got punished by these women for being too available. Some of you end up telling these women how you feel about them and they tell you "let's remain as friends" "you're like a brother to me" because you don't know when to STFU. You've always been available typing shitt in her dm, making her feel too comfortable around you and nothing about you looks attractive anymore. You're just that guy she can always use to kiII boredom. She doesn't see you beyond that. Ask yourself why women have male best friends, guys who fooIishIy entertain people's girlfriends, guys in the friendzone they use for attention, the comedians who crack jokes in their DMs, they got chat buddies and keep them around until it's time to get their back blown and she knows who to go and meet, not one of those jobless boys who text her all day, but the busy man who focuses on his grind and texts her when necessary. If someone's girlfriend is using you this way, pity your mom, Go get a job. The more available you are, the drier she gets down there for you. Take your life seriously, create a habit that keeps you busy daily, not only will you become more attractive that way, you also get a better life, and you'd stop putting yourself in the position to be used by these women. Some of you, these women ignore your messages all day, until she's bored, then she texts you and you respond with the speed of light, she then continuously use you for attention since her man is busy with his life, but you who's not purposeful is always available. It's even more ridiculous when a girI always text you knowing the response she'll get from you when she ask "are you free?" or "what are you doing?" is "I'm free" or "nothing" respectively. You don't expect such women to take you seriously, they see you as jobless. When these women ask you "what you doing?" Sometimes, be ashamed of your jobIessness, even when you're doing nothing, tell her you're busy, tell her "I'll text you in 30 minutes, I'm busy with a project for now" "give me 1hour, I got works to do at the moment". Have some shame!!! Finally, the best way to handle too much availability is by truly getting busy, do something productive with your life. Have a daily routine that keeps you occupied; That way, you don't have to stress about being too available, your daily routine won't permit it. Save yourself, Kings."""

Dataset context:
- Creator: Wizarab
- Video: Child Support and Divorce
- Matched keywords: equal; man

Comment:
"""I once knew a British-Spanish man who shared his experience with me a few years ago. After separating from the mother of his child, he was paying a large amount in child support. Eventually, he decided he had had enough and took the matter to court to fight for 50/50 custody. In the end, the court granted equal custody. Because of that arrangement, he no longer pays child support to her. Each of them is responsible for their own expenses and for taking care of the child during the time the child stays with them. She covers her rent and the child's needs when the child is with her, and he does the same when the child is with him. The only extra responsibility he still takes on is covering the child's school related expenses. What also stood out to me was how structured everything became afterward. They do not communicate directly with each other. Instead, they use an app to manage communication. At a specific time each day, when the child is with the other parent, he places a call through the app and speaks only with his child for about fifteen minutes and then the app shuts them out automatically because it's set for that specific time duration. Even the drop offs are handled in a very controlled way. They meet at a designated public location, he hands the child over to her without speaking to her, and the same happens when she returns the child to him. I admired the way he stood up for himself and worked toward an arrangement that he felt was fair and peaceful."""
